learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of teaching as a dynamic and challenging form of professional work.

Demonstrate an understanding of the diverse backgrounds, development and characteristics of students, and how these may affect learning, and teaching strategies for supporting them in their personal development, health and wellbeing.
Identify and implement teaching strategies, including the use of ICTs, that provide students with appropriate, challenging learning goals and an engaging yet safe and supportive learning environment.

Recognise and understand the place of legislative, school and employing authority requirements in the teaching and learning process, and which enable strategies to support student well-being and safety.

Demonstrate knowledge of relevant codes of practices and critical reflection on the technical and communication skills required to interact effectively with students, care givers and fellow professionals in an educational environment.
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.
Demonstrate all teaching and professional requirements of the professional experience placement.

assessments.assessment_title: Task 1: Policies and procedures for professional experience placement
assessments.task_description:
This course requires you to complete 10 days (a 2 week block) of professional experience, in a school assigned to you by the UQ School of Education.

To undertake this placement, you need to:

  1. Read and ensure you are familiar with the School of Education’s Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements document.
  2. Complete and submit the declaration statement via Blackboard, indicating that you have read this document and will abide by these policies and procedures, while representing the University of Queensland on placement.

Focusing Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs) for this assessment — Graduate career stage: 7.1 and 7.2.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Access and read the Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements document, then complete and submit the online declaration via the course Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of this assessment will result in a failing grade and withdrawal of the professional experience placement.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Write a critical reflection essay on a topic of interest that was covered by the four-week module on teacher and student health and wellbeing. 

Your essay should be 1500 words and critically reflect:

  • on a topic within the area or areas listed below
  • on how the topic has contributed to building your professional knowledge as a pre-service teacher
  • by referring to content covered in the module, relevant academic literature, and the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers at the Graduate career stage (in particular, APSTs 4.4, 7.1 and 7.2).

Child protection

  • Curriculum approaches to support child protection strategies
  • Impact of child protection policies on teachers’ health and wellbeing
  • Exploring the boundaries between caring teaching and child welfare

Community partnerships for health and wellbeing

  • Empowering parents and carers to support young people’s wellbeing
  • Working with external providers effectively to promote student health and wellbeing
  • Teachers’ work as boundary spanners in the provision of school health and welfare services

Students’ mental health and wellbeing

  • Teachers’ role in modelling positive health and wellbeing for students
  • School resources that support students positive health and wellbeing
  • Effective policy landscape for the promotion of student health and wellbeing

Acknowledging sources

Use of APA7 referencing conventions is required, including a completed generative AI / machine translation (MT) technologies statement to the template provided in Blackboard.

Please note, if you use generative AI and/or MT technologies in any way in your assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference use of generative AI or MT technology may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate use, referencing and acknowledgement are available via the UQ Library Guide Overview: AI tools for assignments and AI Student Hub: UQ rules for using AI in assessment.

Focusing Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs) for this assessment — Graduate career stage: 4.4, 7.1, 7.2
assessments.submission_guidelines: Via Blackboard, upload to TurnitIn by 2pm on the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission without an approved extension request via myUQ will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Task 3: Professional experience placement and final report
assessments.task_description:
This course requires you to:

  • successfully undertake a 10 day block (two weeks) of supervised professional experience, at a school assigned to you by the UQ School of Education
  • at the end of your placement, upload your school-completed final report to the course Blackboard, signed and dated by you, your School Coordinator and Teacher Supervisor/s.

As detailed in the UQ School of Education Professional Experience Handbook, the following observation and teaching load requirements apply for this placement:

  • Your first 2 to 3 days are to be observation (i.e., “Hands-on” observation helping the supervising teacher and students).
  • During the remaining days, you can start teaching segments of lessons (e.g., 10 mins or 15 mins, half a lesson– this might be the start of the lesson, the conclusion, a lesson quiz, discussion, group activity, explaining a concept, etc.).
  • If comfortable with lesson segments, you can progress to one full lesson. 

You need to have read and abide by the UQ School of Education's Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements, including attendance and completion requirements.

Further information and resources are provided on the UQ School of Education PEx site for Students. Targeted resources and activities to support your preparation will be provided through course workshops and in in the 'Professional Experience' section of Blackboard.

Please note, as a pre-service teacher, you are responsible for the collection and upload of your own school-completed final report on the last day of your placement. Failure to upload your completed form by the due date will result in a failing grade for the course.

Focusing Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs) for this assessment — Graduate career stage:  1.1, 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, 2.6 3.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4 6.3, 7.1 and 7.2
assessments.submission_guidelines: Upload your final report via Blackboard by 2pm on your last day of placement.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Collate and present a portfolio of your 10 day professional experience placement, containing a summary of your observations; examples of and personal reflections on your planning, including professional goals for your next placement; and your personal teaching philosophy.

Part 1: Summary of observations

Write a 2000-word summary of your observations from placement that addresses each of the following topics:

  • Understanding the school environment and being a teacher
  • Knowing your learners (physical, social and intellectual development and characteristics of students, and how these may affect learning)
  • Strategies to support full participation of students with disability
  • Literacy and numeracy teaching strategies and/or programs, and their application in the classroom and school context
  • Practical approaches to manage challenging behaviour
  • Assessment (systemic data – school, class, focus on students – for example PATM, PATR, NAPLAN; moderation of assessment and reporting mechanisms; the purpose of keeping accurate and reliable records of achievement).

Your summary should refer to your observation notes from placement and be organised under the following headings (focus areas):

  • An introduction to the school environment: who are the various stakeholders?
  • School policies and procedures: legislative, administrative and organisational
  • The importance of communication within a school community
  • Extracurricular activities and opportunities within a school
  • The duties and responsibilities of being a teacher
  • Who are your learners? Understanding, catering for and managing individual student needs, and supporting student health and wellbeing
  • An introduction to classroom management
  • Developing literacy and numeracy skills at the classroom and school level
  • Organising learning: teaching and learning strategies and activities – the what, the why and how
  • Use of information communication and technologies (ICTs) in the classroom and across the school.

Part 2: Planning, reflections and goals

Part 2 of your portfolio requires you to provide and reflect on your own planning and delivery of lesson segments, mini lessons, and full lessons (where possible), from your professional experience placement.

  1. From your placement, provide three plans that you created and taught, including resources.
  2. For each plan, write a 150-200 word personal reflection, addressing what went well and what you would improve for next time.
  3. Using your personal reflections, and feedback you received from supervising teacher(s), identify three professional learning goals that will guide your next professional experience placement. These goals should be linked to relevant Australian Professional Standards for Teachers and their focus areas, at the Graduate career stage.

Part 3: Personal teaching philosophy

Write a 500 word personal teaching philosophy that captures how you aim to ensure that your teaching has a positive impact on student learning.

This will be an important and valuable statement to revisit as your progress through professional experience courses and your program.

Write your philosophy:

  • through reflection on your professional experience placement for this course and your journey in MTeach (Secondary) program to date
  • with reference to what you have prepared for Parts 1 and 2 of this task
  • identifying your values, beliefs and ideas about teaching and the type of teacher you aim to become.

The three domains of the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers may help you frame your philosophy.

Acknowledging sources

  • You may refer to sources in your portfolio (e.g., theories, readings, websites, other published resources). However, please note that this is not the focus of this task.
  • Any use of sources requires use of APA7 referencing conventions.
  • You must complete and include a generative AI/machine translation technology statement, using the template provided in Blackboard.

Please note, if you use generative AI and/or MT technologies in any way in your assessment task, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference use of generative AI or MT technology may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate use, referencing and acknowledgement are available via the UQ Library Guide Overview: AI tools for assignments and AI Student Hub: UQ rules for using AI in assessment.

Focusing Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APSTs) for this assessment — Graduate career stage: 1.1, 1.6, 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, 4.3, 4.4, 6.1 and 6.3

learning_activities.topic:
An introduction to being a teacher and school life

This week's workshop and course readings introduce you to:

  • key aspects of teaching and school life — we'll look at the role of a teacher and consider the qualities of a good teacher, and policies and standards that guide teaching practices, such as the QCT Code of Ethics, legislative, school and employing authority requirements, and ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process. We'll also discuss the nature of the school environment (who are the various stakeholders?)
  • classroom observation skills and how to prepare for school visits — we'll look at school visit protocols and discuss how to work effectively with supervising teachers and respond appropriately to feedback, designed to improve teaching practice
  • requirements for workshop microteaching activities.

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.

Focusing APSTs: 3.5, 4.1, 7.1 and 7.2

Learning outcomes: L01, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Know students and how they learn

This week we'll focus on understanding the physical, social and intellectual development and characteristics of students, and how these may affect learning, as an essential skill for all pre-service teachers. We'll also discuss the implications of student development and characteristics for teaching, in preparation for your professional experience placement.

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.

>> Microteaching activities

Focusing APSTs: 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 4.1, 7.1 and 7.2

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 6(10 Aug - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Teacher and student health and wellbeing

This four-week module of workshops focuses on pre-service teachers' health knowledge (e.g. child protection, community partnerships in health, students' mental health and wellbeing, and maintaining teachers' own health and well-being).

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.

Focusing APSTs: 4.4, 7.1 and 7.2


Learning outcomes: L02, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Planning for and implementing effective teaching and learning

This week we turn our attention to lesson planning for effective teaching and learning. You'll learn how to identify learning goals and objectives for student learning, make links to curriculum requirements, and develop your knowledge of how students learn. There'll also be opportunities to become familiar with the system of education in Queensland and navigating curriculum documents.

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.

>> Microteaching activities

Focusing APSTs: 2.1 - 2.6 and 3.1 - 3.7

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06

An introduction to classroom organisation and behaviour management

Over these two workshops, we'll discuss classroom organisation, routines and strategies, and you'll be introduced to practical approaches for managing challenging behaviours. We'll also revisit lesson planning and expectations for the professional experience placement in Weeks 10 and 11.

learning_activities.topic:
Reflecting on planning for & implementing effective teaching & learning

As you return from your professional experiences, we'll reflect on your placements with a focus on lesson planning; teaching strategies for learning activities; using the physical layout of learning spaces; and strategies for developing literacy and numeracy skills at the classroom and school levels.

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.

>> Microteaching activities

Focusing APSTs: 3.1 to 3.7

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

Professional learning — where to from here?

In our final week, we'll consider professional learning needs and goals for your continuous growth and development as pre-service teachers, and the importance of making connections between ongoing teaching development and the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers.

Please check Blackboard for weekly course readings.
